# ATP

ATP (adenosine triphosphate) is the primary energy currency of the cell. It consists of
adenine, ribose, and three phosphate groups linked by high-energy phosphoanhydride bonds.

Hydrolysis of the terminal phosphate bond releases approximately 30.5 kJ/mol under standard
conditions, providing free energy to drive endergonic biological reactions such as muscle
contraction, biosynthesis, and active transport.

ATP is continuously regenerated from ADP by cellular respiration (oxidative phosphorylation,
glycolysis, and the Krebs cycle) and by phosphocreatine in muscle tissue.
